item.title: US sanctions Cuba’s state oil company amid continued pressure campaign
item.description: Cupet draws latest target as Trump continues to turn up heat on Caribbean nation

item.title: Trump announces Iran deal, calls off new bombing campaign
item.description: US president claims an agreement with Iran to end the war will be signed, though details are scant

item.title: SLB eyes ‘digital transformation’ of Venezuela’s oil sector after PDVSA deal
item.description: Oilfield contractor, which maintained a presence in country, signs long-term agreement to help modernise aging oil infrastructure

item.title: Trump threatens Iran's Kharg Island, promises more strikes
item.description: US president said he is intent on seizing Iran's oil infrastructure 'much like we have with Venezuela'

item.title: Ocean Installer wins multi-year contract with Equinor offshore Brazil
item.description: Bacalhau pre-salt field is Equinor's largest international asset

item.title: Iraqi Oil Ministry vows to fight corruption as deputy minister detained
item.description: Arrest of Adnan Al-Jumaili comes amid unfolding fuel supply crisis in the country

item.title: Another major shareholder divests from Norwegian contractor
item.description: SED Energy is a combination of the tender assist drilling company Energy Holdings and the geophysical company Seabird Exploration

item.title: UK North Sea producer continues its run of M&A
item.description: Serica has completed multiple M&A deals since 2023

item.title: Oil prices up on new US threats as Trump claims 100 million barrels sneaked through Hormuz
item.description: US now gearing up for more bombing after Trump revealed a 'secret mission' to slip oil tankers past Iran undetected

item.title: Trump claims 100 million barrels sneaked through Hormuz
item.description: US now gearing up for more bombing after Trump revealed a 'secret mission' to slip oil tankers past Iran undetected

item.title: Malaysia firms up LNG supply to Japan
item.description: Japan ramping up efforts to secure gas supplies as Middle East conflict continues

item.title: Duo of Malaysian FSRUs progress
item.description: Malaysia pushing ahead two LNG import projects targeting commercial operations in 2029

item.title: Petronas highlights key terms of EnQuest’s $833 million farm-ins
item.description: UK independent boosting Malaysian upstream portfolio with three key deals

item.title: Saipem-NSH joint venture wins massive contract for Saudi Arabia gas project
item.description: Partnership lands $1.04 billion deal for Uthmaniyah gas compression plant expansion

item.title: Energy flows could take ‘many months’ to stabilise: US energy chief
item.description: Despite war’s energy pains, Chris Wright sees brighter future ahead for global industry

item.title: Germany to launch final LNG import terminal approved after Russian gas price shock
item.description: Floating LNG terminal in Stade will start operations in September after two-year delay

item.title: APA to buy Alaska player in North Slope push
item.description: Acquisition of Savant Alaska gives APA and partners a producing asset and access to Trans-Alaska Pipeline

item.title: Petrobras strikes deal with Equinor for 50% stake in Brazil pre-salt area
item.description: Companies already partner in Campos basin in projects such as Raia and Jaspe

item.title: Occidental takes 10% stake in Trinidad & Tobago offshore block
item.description: Operator ExxonMobil is concluding 3D seismic survey in Block TTUD-1

item.title: Devon hints at asset sales following $58 billion merger close
item.description: Portfolio review ‘underway’ after Coterra merger, US shale player says

item.title: MegaSurvey move: TGS agrees key Wesrt Africa seismic deal
item.description: Contractor will harmonise Equatorial Guinea seismic data to de-risk subsurface

item.title: MegaSurvey move: TGS agrees key West Africa seismic deal
item.description: Contractor will harmonise Equatorial Guinea seismic data to de-risk subsurface

item.title: Seplat chief executive to step down next month
item.description: Roger Brown helmed Nigerian independent during its challenging acquisition of ExxonMobil's assets

item.title: German player continues to sell down stake in Harbour Energy
item.description: BASF initially had a 47% shareholding following Wintershall Dea acquisition

item.title: EnQuest eyes $800 million boost to Malaysia portfolio
item.description: UK independent's proposed deal enhances its strategic partnership with national upstream company Petronas Carigali

item.title: Chevron is steering crude flows to Asia and Europe amid Middle East war: CFO
item.description: About 5% of Chevron's global production is impacted, according to Eimear Bonner

item.title: Two-horse race for New Zealand LNG project
item.description: Government progressing liquefied natural gas import scheme to boost energy security

item.title: Samsung Heavy Industries in direct talks for two more FLNG vessels
item.description: South Korean heavyweight already awarded first liquefaction facility for Delfin LNG

item.title: PV Drilling acquires jack-up rig to meet ‘regional drilling demand’
item.description: Vietnamese contractor expects rising domestic and international exploration activity

item.title: Inpex moves to halt escalating strike action at Ichthys LNG project
item.description: Workers have already downed tools at 9.3 million tonnes per annum Australian liquefied natural gas project
